purpose: Shared cross-language fixture for token/cost accounting, budget enforcement, and failure attribution semantics. Preview runtimes must execute these semantics before claiming runtime-core parity.

required_scenarios.name: tool_and_model_cost_attributed_to_run_step_role
required_scenarios.description: Tool and model usage records should be attributed to run, session, step, agent role, tool/model name, amount, and unit.

required_scenarios.name: budget_exhaustion_blocks_execution
required_scenarios.description: A runtime budget limit should block model or tool execution before additional cost is created.

required_scenarios.name: failure_attribution_classifies_agent_tool_model_runtime
required_scenarios.description: Failures should be classified by source and linked to run, step, tool/model, retryability, and final step status.

required_scenarios.name: runtime_model_evidence_boundary
required_scenarios.description: Externally executed model calls should be recorded as replayable evidence without requiring runtime-core to route provider traffic.

preview_status: implemented by Go, TypeScript, and Rust preview runtimes as runtime-core parity gates; production adapter hardening remains separate
